title: Google OAuth2 redirect fails with localhost error
symptom: After granting permissions during Google OAuth2 authentication, the user is redirected to an error page stating 'localhost refused to connect'.
applies_when: n8n is hosted on a platform like Render.com
not_this_if: Using a custom domain or reverse proxy
root_cause: The error occurs because 'localhost' is not accessible from the internet. When Google attempts to redirect back to the specified localhost URL, it fails since that address is not reachable from Google's servers.
fix_steps: Set up n8n on a fully qualified domain name (FQDN)
Update the OAuth Redirect URL in the Google Cloud Console to match the new domain
agent_instruction: Configure n8n with a proper domain name instead of localhost for OAuth2 authentication.
